milestone: Mapped the selector 0x107 OTL handler gates, the fall-through path to 0x1401f6d51, and the local string/object lifetimes consumed by the handler.

arguments_from_dispatcher.role: client/login context object used through RDI/RCX fields
stack-like script/value frame consumed by the handler prolog

handler_entry_gates.meaning: Consumes one queued/frame record, selects a 0x20-byte record under RDX+0x10a8, and requires selected_record+0x18 == 2.
Consumes one script/frame stack entry and requires the indexed word/dword at RDX+RAX*4+0x100 to equal 1.

failure.effect: Normal return without entering the OTL consumer arm.

client_state_classifier.decision: Only computed state class 1 reaches 0x1401f6d51; all other classes branch to normal return at 0x1401f6fd4.

classes.meaning: A nonzero first byte/field in the object at client+0x536b8 enables the OTL arm.
client+0x19f28 == 7 diverts away from this arm.
client+0x19f28 == 8 diverts away from this arm.
A present client+0x198d0 child with byte +0x169 set diverts away from this arm.

final_gate: 0x1401f6d51 is fall-through, not a direct branch target

client_string_source.meaning: Builds a local string at RBP+0x27 from an inline-or-heap small-string style field on the client object.

login_child_string_source.path: client+0x198d0 -> +0x110 -> string at +0x38/+0x40 with flag byte +0x4f
login_child_string_source.meaning: Copies a child small-string into local RBP+0x0f, clears/resets the original child string with 0x140031b10 and a zero write to R14+0.

selected_frame_string_source.meaning: Uses the selected queued/frame record as another inline-or-heap string input.

url_format_and_sink.meaning: Formats an OTL URL-style string from the client string, the selected frame string, and the child login string, then passes the formatted local string to the object at [client+0x536b8]+0x8.

cleanup_sites.meaning: Formatted URL local string cleanup when heap-backed.
Copied child string cleanup when heap-backed.
Copied client string cleanup when heap-backed.

cleanup_lifetimes.vtable_call: R10 loaded from [selected_manager_object_vtable+0x20], then CALL R10

tool_progress.purpose: Restrict exact field-displacement scans to explicit function ranges or .pdata-resolved functions, which keeps handler-local structure maps small and repeatable.
decoder_coverage_added: 0x80 byte immediate-group memory operands, used by CMP [RAX+0x169], 0x0
0x81 and 0x83 immediate-group memory operands for future exact field gates

opennxt_947_implications: The OpenNXT-side model should treat selector 0x107 as the outer dispatch event, while 0x1401f6d51 is an internal fall-through arm reached only after the handler consumes and validates frame inputs.
The OTL path appears to require a state/object readiness flag under client+0x536b8 before URL construction; client+0x19f28 values 7/8 and child byte +0x169 are explicit divert states.

The URL construction evidence ties this handler to the steam/OTL handoff string shape `%s%s?steamMode=runescape&otlToken=%s`; future server/client parity work should correlate the three format inputs with OpenNXT login bootstrap fields before naming them permanently.

The handler resets or consumes the child login string at client+0x198d0 -> +0x110 after copying it, so server replay/parity tests should account for one-shot token lifetime behavior rather than assuming a durable field.
next_questions: Name the three `%s` inputs to `%s%s?steamMode=runescape&otlToken=%s` by tracing the selected frame record and the client+0x19da8 producer.
Map the type/value stack rooted at RDX+0x10a8 and RDX+0x10a0 so selector 0x107 can be represented as a stable OpenNXT-side event schema.
Identify 0x1402984d0 and the [client+0x536b8]+0x8 sink object so the URL handoff side effect is named rather than just address-labeled.
